key_insight: A frozen CT-CLIP foundation model paired with a lightweight survival head can improve lung cancer survival prediction from pretreatment CT plus clinical data in a small cohort.
clinical_relevance: This suggests domain-specific multimodal AI may help identify higher- and lower-risk patients for prognosis support when curated outcome data are limited.
method: Evaluated CT-CLIP as a feature extractor for pretreatment CT images and clinical variables.
Compared frozen encoders, full fine-tuning, and low-rank adaptation.
Ran modality ablations and benchmarked against clinical and multimodal baselines.
evidence_signal: The study analyzed 242 diagnosed lung cancer patients and found the frozen CT-CLIP approach outperformed the clinical baseline and was comparable to or better than other multimodal methods.
watch_item: The dataset is relatively small and retrospective, so performance and risk-group separation should be validated externally before clinical use.
paper_title: CT-CLIP Representations for Multimodal Lung Cancer Survival Prediction
